reason: Irreconcilable conflict between behavior_in_task_description and no_solution_leakage caused by test design. Tests assert on exact gold-specific implementation strings (variable names STAT_FMT_FLAG, STAT_FMT, LARGE_FILE_WHITELIST; exact expression MAX_FILE_SIZE=$((5 * 1024 * 1024)); exact probe stat -c '%s %n' /dev/null) via text-matching. Including these in instruction.md passes behavior_in_task_description but fails no_solution_leakage. Removing them passes no_solution_leakage but fails behavior_in_task_description and makes the task unsolvable. The root cause is test problems: solution_uniqueness_guard, tests_verify_behavior_not_text, and test_not_tautological all fail because tests match exact implementation text rather than verifying behavioral outcomes. These test problems directly cause the instruction rubric failures (no_solution_leakage, instruction_no_hint_leakage). Oracle verified: nop=0, gold=1.
